problem_statement.title: avoid too large case weights in brier_class()
problem_statement.body:
to close #614

``` r
library("yardstick")
library("hardhat")

# Minimal test data (6 rows) that reproduces the bug
# Pardon the weirdness of the data, I excerpted it from my real dataset.
test_data <- structure(list(market_desc = structure(c(2L, 1L, 2L, 1L, 2L, 
                                                      3L), levels = c("Jumbo", "Large", "Medium", "Small"), label = "MARKET_DESC", class = "factor"), 
                            .pred_Jumbo = c(0.0175137900996568, 0.905673347893833, 0.180545683835453, 
                                            0.848162698412699, 0.307261965811966, 0.114002136752137), 
                            .pred_Large = c(0.134188003615333, 0.0939579803556534, 0.818991960147227, 
                                            0.136926984126984, 0.59858021978022, 0.36792673992674), .pred_Medium = c(0.830772931559736, 
                                                                                                                     0.000368671750513363, 0.000462356017320014, 0.0149103174603175, 
                                                                                                                     0.0941578144078144, 0.517671123321123), .pred_Small = c(0.0175252747252747, 
                                                                                                                                                                             0, 0, 0, 0, 4e-04), weighting = structure(c(5L, 3000L, 1400L, 
                                                                                                                                                                                                                         6L, 22L, 28L), class = c("hardhat_frequency_weights", "hardhat_case_weights", 
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  "vctrs_vctr"))), row.names = c(NA, -6L), class = c("tbl_df", 
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     "tbl", "data.frame"))

# TEST 1: brier_class WITHOUT case_weights - WORKS
brier_class(
  test_data,
  truth = market_desc,
  .pred_Jumbo:.pred_Small
)
#> # A tibble: 1 × 3
#>   .metric     .estimator .estimate
#>   <chr>       <chr>          <dbl>
#> 1 brier_class multiclass     0.184


# TEST 2: brier_class WITH case_weights - RETURNS NaN (BUG)
brier_class(
  test_data,
  truth = market_desc,
  .pred_Jumbo:.pred_Small,
  case_weights = weighting
)
#> # A tibble: 1 × 3
#>   .metric     .estimator .estimate
#>   <chr>       <chr>          <dbl>
#> 1 brier_class multiclass   0.00886
```

<sup>Created on 2026-01-27 with [reprex v2.1.1](https://reprex.tidyverse.org)</sup>
problem_statement.issue_title: brier_class() returns NaN for extreme values of case_weights
problem_statement.issue_body:
## The problem

`brier_class()` returns `NaN` when using extreme values of `case_weights`, while other probability-based metrics like `mn_log_loss()` correctly handle case weights. 

This causes `brier_class()` to fail silently in `tune_grid()` workflows that use case weights, returning `n=0` in `collect_metrics()`.


## Reproducible Example
``` r
library("yardstick")
library("hardhat")

# Minimal test data (6 rows) that reproduces the bug
# Pardon the weirdness of the data, I excerpted it from my real dataset.
test_data <- structure(list(market_desc = structure(c(2L, 1L, 2L, 1L, 2L, 
                                                      3L), levels = c("Jumbo", "Large", "Medium", "Small"), label = "MARKET_DESC", class = "factor"), 
                            .pred_Jumbo = c(0.0175137900996568, 0.905673347893833, 0.180545683835453, 
                                            0.848162698412699, 0.307261965811966, 0.114002136752137), 
                            .pred_Large = c(0.134188003615333, 0.0939579803556534, 0.818991960147227, 
                                            0.136926984126984, 0.59858021978022, 0.36792673992674), .pred_Medium = c(0.830772931559736, 
                                                                                                                     0.000368671750513363, 0.000462356017320014, 0.0149103174603175, 
                                                                                                                     0.0941578144078144, 0.517671123321123), .pred_Small = c(0.0175252747252747, 
                                                                                                                                                                             0, 0, 0, 0, 4e-04), weighting = structure(c(5L, 3000L, 1400L, 
                                                                                                                                                                                                                         6L, 22L, 28L), class = c("hardhat_frequency_weights", "hardhat_case_weights", 
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  "vctrs_vctr"))), row.names = c(NA, -6L), class = c("tbl_df", 
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     "tbl", "data.frame"))


levels(test_data$market_desc)
#> [1] "Jumbo"  "Large"  "Medium" "Small"



# TEST 1: brier_class WITHOUT case_weights - WORKS
brier_no_weights <- brier_class(
  test_data,
  truth = market_desc,
  .pred_Jumbo:.pred_Small
)
print("Test 1 - brier_class WITHOUT case_weights:")
#> [1] "Test 1 - brier_class WITHOUT case_weights:"
print(brier_no_weights)
#> # A tibble: 1 × 3
#>   .metric     .estimator .estimate
#>   <chr>       <chr>          <dbl>
#> 1 brier_class multiclass     0.184


# TEST 2: brier_class WITH case_weights - RETURNS NaN (BUG)
brier_with_weights <- brier_class(
  test_data,
  truth = market_desc,
  .pred_Jumbo:.pred_Small,
  case_weights = weighting
)

print("\nTest 2 - brier_class WITH case_weights:")
#> [1] "\nTest 2 - brier_class WITH case_weights:"
print(brier_with_weights)
#> # A tibble: 1 × 3
#>   .metric     .estimator .estimate
#>   <chr>       <chr>          <dbl>
#> 1 brier_class multiclass       NaN

# TEST 3: mn_log_loss WITH case_weights - WORKS (for comparison)
logloss_with_weights <- mn_log_loss(
  test_data,
  truth = market_desc,
  .pred_Jumbo:.pred_Small,
  case_weights = weighting
)
print("\nTest 3 - mn_log_loss WITH case_weights (comparison):")
#> [1] "\nTest 3 - mn_log_loss WITH case_weights (comparison):"
print(logloss_with_weights)
#> # A tibble: 1 × 3
#>   .metric     .estimator .estimate
#>   <chr>       <chr>          <dbl>
#> 1 mn_log_loss multiclass     0.138

sessionInfo()
#> R version 4.5.2 (2025-10-31 ucrt)
#> Platform: x86_64-w64-mingw32/x64
#> Running under: Windows 11 x64 (build 26200)

#> [5] LC_TIME=English_United States.utf8    
#> 
#> time zone: America/New_York
```

patch:
diff --git a/NEWS.md b/NEWS.md
index cbae4120..cfd88a63 100644
--- a/NEWS.md
+++ b/NEWS.md
@@ -18,6 +18,8 @@
 
 * The ranked probability score for ordinal classification data was added with `ranked_prob_score()`. (#524)
 
+* Fixed bug where `brier_class()` returns NaN with extreme value case weights. (#614)
+
 * `poisson_log_loss()` has been enhanced to handle 0 valued estimates, no longer returning `Inf` or `NaN`. (#513)
 
 * Fixed bug where ranked probability metrics didn't work in combination with other classification metrics in `metric_set()`. (#539)
diff --git a/R/prob-brier_class.R b/R/prob-brier_class.R
index 5d0a5125..e06c5bce 100644
--- a/R/prob-brier_class.R
+++ b/R/prob-brier_class.R
@@ -160,6 +160,11 @@ brier_ind <- function(truth, estimate, case_weights = NULL) {
   case_weights <- case_weights[not_missing]
 
   # Normalize weights (in case negative weights)
+  # subtracting max to avoid Inf in calculations
+  #  exp(x - max(x)) / sum(exp(x - max(x)))
+  #  = (exp(x) / exp(max(x))) / (sum(exp(x)) / exp(max(x)))
+  #  = exp(x) / sum(exp(x))
+  case_weights <- case_weights - max(case_weights)
   case_weights <- exp(case_weights) / sum(exp(case_weights))
 
   res <- sum(resids * case_weights) / (2 * sum(case_weights))
test_patch:
diff --git a/tests/testthat/test-prob-brier_class.R b/tests/testthat/test-prob-brier_class.R
index a95fbbf4..6e7ba0b8 100644
--- a/tests/testthat/test-prob-brier_class.R
+++ b/tests/testthat/test-prob-brier_class.R
@@ -107,3 +107,15 @@ test_that("range values are correct", {
     expect_gte(brier_class_vec(df$truth, df$off), worst)
   }
 })
+
+test_that("doesn't produce NaN with high valued case weights (#614)", {
+  df <- two_class_example
+
+  wts <- rep(1, (nrow(df)))
+  wts_high <- wts * 5000
+
+  expect_identical(
+    brier_class_vec(df$truth, df$Class1, case_weights = wts),
+    brier_class_vec(df$truth, df$Class1, case_weights = wts_high)
+  )
+})

fail_to_pass: Test that brier_class_vec() with case_weights of 5000 produces identical results to case_weights of 1 (not NaN)
pass_to_pass: Existing brier_class tests for basic functionality
Tests with normal case weights values

key_changes: Subtract max(case_weights) from all weights before applying exp() to avoid overflow
The mathematical identity: exp(x - max(x)) / sum(exp(x - max(x))) = exp(x) / sum(exp(x))
This is the standard log-sum-exp trick for numerical stability
potential_pitfalls: Must ensure the subtraction happens before exp() is called
The fix should not change the mathematical result, only improve numerical stability
Need to handle edge cases like all weights being equal or single observation

instruction: Fix the bug in brier_class() where the function returns NaN when using extreme values of case_weights. The issue occurs in the brier_ind() function in R/prob-brier_class.R where exp(case_weights) produces Inf for large weight values, causing NaN results. The function should produce identical results regardless of the scale of case weights (e.g., weights of 1 vs weights of 5000 should give the same result).
context: The brier_ind() function normalizes case weights using: case_weights <- exp(case_weights) / sum(exp(case_weights)). When case_weights contain large values (e.g., 5000), exp(5000) returns Inf, causing the normalization to produce NaN. This is a numerical overflow issue that can be solved using the standard log-sum-exp trick for numerical stability.
